value: Investigate the effects of alloy composition, cold rolling and heat treatment temperature on concurrent precipitation of κ-carbide/B2 particles, recrystallization, and mechanical properties in Fe-Mn-Al-C-(Ni) austenitic low-density steels.
Effect of cold rolling and subsequent heat treatment on microstructural evolution and mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C-(Ni) based austenitic low-density steels
Nickel-containing low-density steel with lower Mn.
Nickel-containing low-density steel with high Mn.
Homogenized at 1423 K for 2.5 h and hot rolled at 1323 K.
Referred as HR condition. Contains B2 stringers.
Homogenized at 1423 K for 2.5 h, hot rolled at 1323 K, then cold rolled to ~50% reduction in thickness at room temperature.
Referred as HR+CR condition. Experienced cracking beyond 50% reduction.
Homogenized at 1423 K for 2.5 h, hot rolled at 1323 K, cold rolled 50%, then heat treated at 1023 K for 1 h followed by water quenching.

multiple pass cold rolling at room temperature
heat treatment at 973 K for 1 h
deformed grains with parallel planar slip bands
Single-phase deformed austenite with planar slip bands; no deformation twinning or martensite.
Recrystallization fraction: 0.4; twin fraction: 0.1; B2 fraction: 0.05
coarser and inhomogeneous in deformed regions
Partially recrystallized austenite (recrystallization fraction ~0.4) with nm-sized κ-carbides and intergranular B2.
Crack propagates along γ/B2 interphase boundaries, leading to quasi-cleavage fracture.
Intergranular cracks observed; cracking initiated at phase boundaries due to large volume fraction of B2 and κ-carbides.
At 1023 K, high fraction of B2 and κ-carbides at grain boundaries promotes brittle intergranular fracture.
Quasi-cleavage fracture with intergranular cracks along γ/B2 interfaces; microcracks present.
Hardness increased to ~430 VHN after 50% cold reduction.
Evaluate tensile properties at room temperature
All three alloys attained similar hardness (~450 VHN) and strength levels after 50% cold reduction, despite different initial hardness.
B2 stringers in 15Mn5Ni underwent shearing and fragmentation during cold rolling, indicating load sharing by the ordered intermetallic phase.
15Mn5Ni alloy exhibited brittle behavior with <1% elongation when heat treated at 973 K and 1023 K, due to high volume fraction of B2 and κ-carbides along grain boundaries.
In Ni-free LDS, processing temperature window around 973 K is critical to achieve strength-ductility balance; slight increase to 1023 K drastically reduces strength.
Dislocation-twin interaction and dynamic slip band refinement were identified as dominant strain hardening mechanisms in fully recrystallized alloys.
